notes: opencode ships /undo + /redo as in-TUI slash commands with git-snapshot semantics. /undo is an IN-PLACE rewind on the SAME ses_… (the daemon's ProcessOwnedStore source reads the same session id throughout), NOT a fork like codex: it sets the session row's `revert` column to {messageID,snapshot,diff} and restores the working-tree files via Snapshot.restore — it does NOT delete the message/part rows (the TUI simply filters reverted messages by id; the next prompt trims id>=messageID and proceeds). Live-proven (v1.14.50, 2026-05-26): /undo set revert, msg/part counts UNCHANGED, same ses_, no fork, clean post-rewind turn. So the observable arc is: session born ready → turn 1 (ready→working→ready) → turn 2 (ready→working→ready) → user rewinds to the turn-1 checkpoint via /undo (the session stays ready — a rewind is not a turn; setting the revert column writes NO part rows, so no state change distinguishes the rewind from a dwell) → turn 3 from the rewound point (ready→working→ready) on the SAME ses_…. The rewind itself is asserted ONLY as a negative invariant on the dwell window before turn 3 (stays ready; no new session). session_birth anchors to recording start UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn1_start (first working ⇒ the ses_… session) establishes identity and every later phase pins via same_session_as. CAVEAT (metric-drift, NOT a daemon bug, out of scope here): because reverted parts are not deleted, post-rewind cost/token metrics fold the full pre-rewind history — the spec asserts state/session_id/arc only, never message/part counts or metrics. HOW runs differ: assistant wording, per-turn tokens, sub-second timing (model variance). Stable structurally: three ready→working→ready arcs on ONE ses_… session, no new session ever created, a single process_exited, final state ready. The /undo is delivered live via drive-opencode-interactive.sh's run_live() tmux path (generic `slash` step); the recipe routes through run_live() because it carries a slash step.

text: A ses_… session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch (pre-session or ses_… UUID, whichever arrives first); never enters waiting before the first prompt
PID bound to the ses_… session
First prompt → working (binds the ses_… session; the pre-session never enters working)
First reply completes → back to ready; this is the checkpoint the user will rewind to
Second prompt → working again on the same ses_… session
Second reply completes → back to ready

User invokes /undo to rewind to the turn-1 checkpoint: the rewind happens in place on the same ses_… (opencode sets the session row's revert column and restores the working-tree files via a git snapshot; the message/part rows are NOT deleted — they are filtered, then trimmed on the next prompt) — the session stays ready and no new session is minted; setting the revert column writes no part rows, so no state change distinguishes the rewind from a dwell
Third prompt, issued from the rewound point → working again on the SAME ses_… (in-place rewind, not a fork)
Post-rewind reply completes → back to ready; the turn arc is clean from the rewound checkpoint
Agent torn down once after the post-rewind turn → a single process_exited on the ses_… session; state immediately before teardown is ready
